turns.text: Good morning everyone. Thursday standup as promised. I want to move quickly but carefully — we have three decisions to lock today before the 24th council. Let’s go function by function first, then decide.

Product update: permissions bug is fixed and in staging. Import performance hit p95 at 2 minutes 48 seconds on the test dataset, which is inside our new threshold. I’m calling product readiness 85 percent.
Marketing is at 90. Lexicon is approved, customer quote cleared. Landing page is coherent if not polished. Teaser email is drafted and scheduled pending your sign-off, Chris.
Sales is at 75. Got the pricing one-pager this morning, thank you Marco and Jen. Battlecards still thin on the competitive side but reps can work with what we have.
Support is at 70. Sandbox is live, top 20 FAQs are answered. Escalation matrix needs one more pass but it’s close.
Legal at 85. Claims inventory done. Contract addendum language is with counterparty counsel.
Ops at 75. Cohort forecast from Caleb was helpful. We’re ready for up to sixty accounts. Exception criteria doc is drafted — Caleb and I aligned on language this morning.

Good progress across the board. First decision: do we formally adopt the updated go/no-go criteria from Tuesday as the binding standard for the 24th council? I think yes, but before I call it — Thomas, we haven’t heard your view on whether the p95 import threshold creates any compliance exposure.
No compliance issue with the threshold itself. My concern was always claims language, not performance specs.
Good. Naomi, you flagged ops risk if we set the bar too low. Does p95 under three minutes satisfy you?
It’s acceptable. Not ideal, but acceptable.

Okay. Decision locked: updated go/no-go criteria are the binding standard for the 24th. Everyone agreed? [General agreement] Second decision: teaser email. Jen has a draft. Before I approve it — Rina, support hasn’t weighed in yet. If this goes out Tuesday, are your agents ready for inbound questions?
We need the FAQ live before send, not after.
FAQ can be live Monday morning.
Then yes, we’re ready.
Thomas, any last exposure in the draft you want to flag before I sign off?
I reviewed it this morning. It’s clean.
Teaser email approved for Tuesday send, FAQ live Monday. Locked.
Can we talk about the competitive battlecards? Reps are going into calls underprepared on the two main competitors.
Yes, but let’s finish the structured decisions first and then open floor. Third decision: launch exception policy. Caleb and Naomi have a draft. Caleb, give us the headline.
First-come first-served up to sixty. Up to ten executive-approved exceptions. Criteria are: existing strategic account, multi-year contract prospect, or CEO-flagged relationship.
Before we lock this — Marco, you haven’t said anything on the exception policy. Any product or capacity risk if strategic exceptions push us to seventy or seventy-five accounts?
Onboarding quality drops if we go above sixty-five without additional engineering support on call.
That’s the number I needed. Sixty-five is the real ceiling, not seventy.
So the policy should read: sixty standard, up to five exceptions not to exceed sixty-five total. Caleb, does that work?
Yes, I can live with that.
That’s much cleaner.
Rina, does sixty-five change your support calculus?
It’s tight but manageable if onboarding is phased over the month.
Naomi, can you phase it?
Yes, roughly twenty per week.
Good. Exception policy locked: sixty standard, up to five exceptions, hard ceiling sixty-five, phased twenty per week. Caleb and Naomi update the doc today.
Done.
Okay, open floor. Battlecards, Caleb.
We have good intel on Competitor A from a recent win. Competitor B is harder — they just released a new pricing page and we don’t fully understand their model.
I can get you a feature comparison by end of day. I won’t speculate on their pricing.
I can have a positioning angle drafted by tomorrow morning based on what Marco sends.
That’s all I need.
Rina, anything from support before we close?
One thing. Permission inheritance in nested folders — Marco said he’d put the final behavior in writing after Tuesday’s call. I still don’t have it.
Sending it in the next hour. Genuinely.
Okay. I’ll hold the FAQ on that section until I receive it.
Marco, that’s a hard dependency. Rina needs it to close the FAQ, FAQ needs to be live Monday, teaser goes Tuesday. The chain is tight.
Understood. One hour.
I’ll update the landing page once the FAQ is final so they’re consistent.
Good. Any other blockers anyone hasn’t had a chance to raise?
One small one. The contract addendum uses language from the old lexicon in one clause. I’m fixing it today but wanted it on record.
Noted and on record.
Nothing from me.

Closing summary. Three decisions locked today: go/no-go criteria binding, teaser email approved for Tuesday with FAQ Monday, exception policy at sixty plus five hard ceiling sixty-five. Open items: Marco sends permission inheritance behavior this hour, Jen and Marco deliver battlecard inputs by tomorrow morning, Thomas closes contract addendum today. Next touchpoint is the 24th launch council. I’ll circulate the central checklist update by end of day. Thanks everyone. [End of meeting]
